title: Upper lithospheric structure of the subduction zone offshore of southern Arauco peninsula, Chile, at ~38°S
journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
abstract: A joint interpretation of swath bathymetric, seismic refraction, wide-angle reflection, and multichannel seismic (MCS) data was used to derive a detailed tomographic image of the Nazca–South America subduction zone system offshore southern Arauco peninsula at ~38°S. Data were acquired during R/V SONNE cruises SO161 (SPOC) and SO181 (TIPTEQ) projects. Seismic reflection data revealed outer rise bending-related faults cutting at least 6 km into the uppermost mantle. The study images the full subduction zone with a 40 km wide accretionary prism, varying crustal structure, and the geometry of the subducting Nazca plate.

location.description: Offshore southern Arauco peninsula at ~38°S, central Chile subduction zone (SPOC/TIPTEQ experiment area)

seismic_lines.name: TIPTEQ Profile P09 — OBS wide-angle refraction (~38°S, 215 km, SO181)
SPOC Profile P01 — OBS wide-angle refraction (~38°S, SO161, 2001)
SPOC MCS Profile P09 — multichannel seismic reflection (~38°S, SO161)

data.name: SPOC/TIPTEQ Wide-angle OBS Seismic Refraction Data (~38°S)
data.description: Wide-angle OBS seismic refraction data from SPOC (SO161) and TIPTEQ (SO181) cruises offshore southern Arauco peninsula at ~38°S. P-wave tomographic velocity model derived.

data.name: SPOC/TIPTEQ Multichannel Seismic Reflection Data (~38°S)
data.description: MCS reflection data from SPOC/TIPTEQ cruises imaging the outer rise, accretionary prism, and subduction zone at ~38°S.

processing.summary: Joint interpretation of swath bathymetric, wide-angle OBS seismic refraction, and MCS reflection data from SPOC (SO161, 2001) and TIPTEQ (SO181, 2004–2005) cruises at ~38°S offshore Arauco peninsula. OBS data processed through 2D P-wave travel time tomography to derive crustal velocity model. MCS data processed for imaging outer rise faults and accretionary prism geometry. Results integrated to produce comprehensive lithospheric model including outer rise bending faults (≥6 km depth) and 40 km wide accretionary prism.
workflow: 1. Wide-angle OBS data from SO161 and SO181
4. MCS: geometry, velocity analysis, stack, migration
processing.notes: Bronze open access PDF available. Key paper for south-central Chile subduction zone structure at ~38°S. First documentation of outer rise bending faults cutting ≥6 km into mantle in this area.

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. Authors corrected: removed Scherwath and Bialas (not authors — Scherwath thanked in acknowledgments, Bialas cited for OBS instrument), added Reichert, Christian (BGR Hannover). Vessel expanded to SO161 SPOC + SO181 TIPTEQ. source_volume_cui=3124 (SPOC MCS: 20 airguns × 51.2 L total = 3124 cui); TIPTEQ refraction source was 8×8 L G-guns = 64 L = 3905 cui. Profile names corrected to P09 (TIPTEQ, 215 km) + P01 (SPOC). 48 OBH/S stations total. Software: TOMO2D (Korenaga et al. 2000). No data availability statement found; data held at IFM-GEOMAR.
